title: Charmed Life - Chapter 9 (Fragment 3)
text:
Dear Replacement,

I have to leave this terrible place. Nobody understands me. Nobody notices my talents. You will soon see because you are my exact double so you will be a witch too. I have been very clever. They do not know all my resauces. I have found out how to go to another world and I am going there for good. I know I shall be Queen of it because my fortune was told and said so. There are hundreds of other worlds only some are nicer than others, they are formed when there is a big event in History like a battle or an earthquake when the result can be two or more quite diferent things. Both those things hapen but they cannot exist together so the world splits into two worlds witch start to go diferent after that. I know there must be Gwendolens in a lot of worlds but not how many. One of you will come here when I go because when I move it will make an empty space that will suck you in. Do not greive however if your parents still live. Some other Gwendolen will move into your place and pretend to be you because we are all so
clever. You can carry on here making Chrestomanci’s life a misery and I shall be greatful knowing it is in good hands.

Your loving

Gwendolen Chant

PS. Burn this.

PS2. Tell Cat I am quite sorry but he must do what Mr. Nostrum says.

Having read this, Cat knelt wanly beside Janet, knowing he really would never see Gwendolen again. He seemed to be stuck with Janet instead. If you know a person as well as Cat knew Gwendolen, an exact double is hardly good enough. Janet was not a witch. The expressions on her face were nothing like the same. Looking at her now, Cat saw that, where Gwendolen would have been furious at being dragged into another world, Janet was looking as wan as he felt.

“I wonder how Mum and Dad are getting on with my Dear Replacement,” she said wryly. Then she pulled herself together. “Do you mind if I don’t burn this? It’s the only proof I’ve got that I’m not Gwendolen who’s suddenly gone mad and thinks she’s this girl called Janet Chant. May I hide it?”

“It’s your letter,” said Cat.

“And your sister,” said Janet. “God bless her dear little sugar-coated shining soul! Don’t get me wrong, Cat. I admire your sister. She thinks big. You have to admire her! All the same, I wonder if she’s thought of the clever hiding place where I’m going to put her letter. I shall feel better if she hasn’t.”

Janet bounced up in her un-Gwendolen-like way and took the letter over to the gilded dressing table. Cat bounced up and followed her. Janet took hold of the gold-garlanded mirror and swung it towards her on its swivels. The back was plain plywood. She dug her nails under the edge of the plywood and prised. It came free quite easily.

“I do this with my mirror at home,” Janet explained. “It’s a good hiding place—it’s about the one place my parents never think of. Mum and Dad are dears, but they’re terribly nosy. I think it’s because I’m their only one. And I like to be private. I write private stories for my eyes only, and they will try to read them. Oh, purple-spotted dalmatians!”

vocabulary.definition: To regard with respect or warm approval.
vocabulary.example: I admire your sister.

vocabulary.definition: A fight between armed forces.
vocabulary.example: A big event in History like a battle or an earthquake.

vocabulary.definition: Moved quickly up and down or in a lively way.
vocabulary.example: Janet bounced up in her un-Gwendolen-like way.

vocabulary.definition: Intelligent or skillful.
vocabulary.example: I have been very clever.

vocabulary.definition: A smart or skillful secret place to hide something.
vocabulary.example: I wonder if she’s thought of the clever hiding place where I’m going to put her letter.

vocabulary.definition: A person or thing that is exactly like another.
vocabulary.example: You are my exact double so you will be a witch too.

vocabulary.definition: A sudden and violent shaking of the ground.
vocabulary.example: A big event in History like a battle or an earthquake.

vocabulary.definition: An occurrence, especially one of importance.
vocabulary.example: A big event in History like a battle or an earthquake.

vocabulary.definition: Accurate or precise; identical in form or character.
vocabulary.example: You are my exact double so you will be a witch too.

vocabulary.definition: To be present or real; to live or survive.
vocabulary.example: They cannot exist together.

vocabulary.definition: To bring together parts into a whole; to create or establish.
vocabulary.example: They are formed when there is a big event in History.

vocabulary.definition: A large amount of wealth or the chance happening of events.
vocabulary.example: I know I shall be Queen of it because my fortune was told.

vocabulary.definition: Covered thinly with gold leaf or gold paint.
vocabulary.example: Janet took the letter over to the gilded dressing table.

vocabulary.definition: To feel or express deep sorrow or distress.
vocabulary.example: Do not grieve however if your parents still live.

vocabulary.definition: Showing too much curiosity about other people's affairs.
vocabulary.example: Mum and Dad are terribly nosy.

vocabulary.definition: A type of strong thin wooden board made from layers glued together.
vocabulary.example: The back was plain plywood.

vocabulary.definition: To act as if something is true when it is not.
vocabulary.example: Some other Gwendolen will pretend to be you.

vocabulary.definition: To move or open something by force or leverage.
vocabulary.example: She dug her nails under the edge of the plywood and prised.

vocabulary.definition: Intended for or restricted to the use of a particular person.
vocabulary.example: I write private stories for my eyes only.

vocabulary.definition: A person or thing that takes the place of another.
vocabulary.example: Dear Replacement,

vocabulary.definition: Sources of help or support, especially in a difficult situation.
vocabulary.example: They do not know all my resources.

vocabulary.definition: A consequence, effect or outcome of something.
vocabulary.example: When the result can be two or more quite different things.

vocabulary.definition: Giving out or reflecting light; bright.
vocabulary.example: God bless her dear little sugar-coated shining soul!

vocabulary.definition: To divide or break into parts.
vocabulary.example: The world splits into two worlds.

vocabulary.definition: Very bad or unpleasant.
vocabulary.example: I have to leave this terrible place.
